reason: The Wave 3 Swift port bound every engine family (reducer, comparator, navigation, completion) to CrossyEngine and drained this list. Wave 15.1 rewrote the check contract to the attributed-majority vote flow (PROTOCOL.md sections 6, 10; DESIGN.md D32) and check was unbound and skipped-until-engine here while the Swift port lacked the vote state machine. Wave 15.5 ported that state machine (CrossyEngine.applyWithVote, the twin of packages/engine/src/completion.ts) and rebound check in Tests/VectorRunnerTests/EngineBindings, so `families` is empty again: every non-foreign family executes against CrossyEngine. A non-foreign family reappears here only if its engine binding is intentionally removed, which the per-family guard in Tests/VectorRunnerTests then flags. Mirrors packages/engine/vectors.skip.json.

foreign.reason: A foreign family's consumer is never CrossyEngine (PROTOCOL.md §13). client-store's consumer is apps/web's store (Wave 2.1d) and the iOS store (Phase I1b); clue-runs' consumer is the clue-run parser and renderer in apps/web and iOS. The runner discovers and shape-validates these vectors but never executes them; execution lives in apps/web's suite and the iOS suites. Unlike `families`, a foreign family is never bound to the engine and never leaves this list, so the Wave 3 per-family rebind that drains `families` must not touch it. Mirrors packages/engine/vectors.skip.json.
